company.name: Alphabet Inc.
company.primary_assets: Servers, Network Equipment, Data Centers, YouTube Content, R&D Facilities

financial_highlights.depreciation_note: FY2023 depreciation expense estimated ~$15B; company disclosed a $3.9B reduction due to useful life extension

accounting_policy.key_change_2023: In January 2023, extended server useful life from 4 to 6 years and network equipment from 5 to 6 years. Effect: $3.9B reduction in depreciation expense for FY2023.
accounting_policy.policy_risk_note: Google proactively extended depreciation lives in 2023, reducing annual depreciation by $3.9B. This is the same pattern as NVIDIA (extending lives to reduce expenses), but Google operates at hyperscale with massive server fleets. The $3.9B benefit flowed directly to profit.

risk_signals.source: Notes to Financial Statements - Note 1 / MD&A
risk_signals.keyword_matched: change in the estimated useful life of our servers and certain network equipment to six years
risk_signals.text_excerpt: In January 2023, we completed an assessment of the useful lives of our servers and network equipment resulting in a change in the estimated useful life of our servers and certain network equipment to six years. The effect of this change was a reduction in depreciation expense of $3.9 billion for the year ended December 31, 2023, recognized primarily in cost of revenues and R&D expenses.
risk_signals.page_location: Note 1. Summary of Significant Accounting Policies / MD&A Cost of Revenues (lines 2120-2125, 2584-2588, 2886-2890)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: This is the strongest signal across all 4 companies so far. Google extended server life from 4 to 6 years and network equipment from 5 to 6 years, directly reducing FY2023 depreciation expense by $3.9B. With AI training clusters using GPUs that iterate every 1-2 years (H100→B100→B200), a 6-year server life assumes the same physical box will generate economic value through 2029. The $3.9B benefit went straight to profit.
evidence_chain: January 2023: Server life extended 4→6 years; network equipment 5→6 years
FY2023 effect: $3.9B reduction in depreciation expense
Benefit recognized in cost of revenues and R&D (flattering both gross margin and operating margin)
GPU technology cycle: H100(2022)→B100(2024)→B200(2025) = 1-2 years
Server life assumption (6 years) >> technology cycle (1-2 years)
If Google replaces servers every 2-3 years for AI, last 3-4 years of depreciation are fictitious
Result: $3.9B profit boost in FY2023 alone; cumulative understatement over 6-year life could exceed $10B

risk_signals.keyword_matched: reduction in depreciation due to the change in estimated useful lives
risk_signals.text_excerpt: cost of revenues benefited from a reduction in depreciation due to the change in estimated useful lives of our servers and network equipment... The $722 million increase in depreciation expense reflected an offsetting benefit of the change in the estimated useful lives of our servers and network equipment.
risk_signals.page_location: MD&A Cost of Revenues (lines 2099-2101); MD&A R&D (lines 2261-2269)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Google explicitly states that cost of revenues AND R&D both benefited from the depreciation life extension. This means the $3.9B benefit hit multiple profit lines: gross profit, operating profit, and net income. The company also notes that without the extension, depreciation expense would have increased by $722M in R&D alone.
evidence_chain: Cost of revenues: benefited from reduced depreciation
R&D expenses: $722M increase would have been larger without the extension
The extension masked underlying growth in depreciation (more servers added)
Profit impact is spread across multiple line items, making it harder to detect
Result: FY2023 profit flattered by $3.9B from accounting change, not operational improvement

risk_signals.keyword_matched: impairment on our marketable and non-marketable securities
risk_signals.text_excerpt: impairment on our marketable and non-marketable securities, performance fees, and income (loss) and impairment from our equity method investments.
risk_signals.page_location: Risk Factors / Notes to Financial Statements (lines 2068-2072, 2508-2527)
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Google holds massive investment portfolio ($30B+ non-marketable securities). While not directly related to fixed asset depreciation, the same macro triggers (recession, tech downturn) that cause investment impairments would also pressure server utilization assumptions embedded in depreciation schedules.
evidence_chain: Google holds large portfolio of non-marketable equity securities
Impairment testing requires judgment on investee financial position
Macro downturn = investment impairments + reduced cloud demand
Reduced cloud demand = lower server utilization = impairment trigger for fixed assets

risk_signals.keyword_matched: impairment of goodwill and/or purchased long-lived assets
risk_signals.text_excerpt: acquisitions and other strategic arrangements could also result in... impairment of goodwill and/or purchased long-lived assets, and restructuring charges, any of which could harm our financial condition and operating results.
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Standard acquisition risk disclosure. Google has made major AI acquisitions (DeepMind, Nest, various AI startups). If acquired technology becomes obsolete faster than amortization schedules assume, impairments may be needed.
evidence_chain: Google has significant goodwill and intangibles from acquisitions
AI technology evolves rapidly; acquired tech may become obsolete
Amortization assumes stable economic benefit over asset life
If AI paradigm shifts, prior acquisitions may lose value

risk_signals.keyword_matched: Any significant impairment of our intellectual property rights could harm our business
risk_signals.text_excerpt: Any significant impairment of our intellectual property rights could harm our business and our ability to compete. Also, protecting our intellectual property rights is costly and time consuming.
risk_signals.relevance_to_depreciation: Google's core IP (search algorithm, ad tech, Android) is not depreciated. This signal is less relevant to fixed asset depreciation, but shows the company acknowledges impairment risk broadly.
evidence_chain: Core IP not on balance sheet (internally developed)
Acquired IP may be impaired if technology shifts
Indirect signal of estimate risk in intangible assets

dimension_scores.dimension_name: 折旧年限 vs 技术实际寿命
dimension_scores.reasoning: 服务器折旧年限从4年延长到6年，网络设备从5年延长到6年。但AI服务器搭载的GPU迭代周期仅1-2年（H100→B100→B200）。Google在2023年主动延长年限，直接减少折旧费用$3.9B，是4家公司中最激进的折旧政策调整。
Google在2023年主动延长折旧年限（4→6年），这是明确的'减少折旧费用、增加利润'方向。效果直接量化：$3.9B利润提升。采用未来适用法（prospectively），历史少提的折旧不补回。这是4家公司中会计政策最不保守的。
Risk Factors提到投资减值和收购相关减值，但没有像Meta那样明确的数据中心资产减值风险。Google的固定资产主要是服务器和网络设备，2023年无大规模减值记录。减值风险相对分散。
FY2023 CAPEX $32.3B，收入$307.4B，CAPEX/收入=10.5%。虽然低于Meta的20.2%，但绝对金额巨大。Google正在大规模扩建AI基础设施（TPU集群、数据中心）。固定资产基数大，折旧假设偏差的杠杆效应强。
Google Cloud面临AWS和Azure的激烈竞争。TPU迭代周期与NVIDIA GPU相当（1-2年）。如果Google的TPU无法跟上NVIDIA的迭代速度，其服务器资产可能提前过时。但Google自研TPU+控制软硬件全栈，技术替代风险略低于纯硬件依赖的Meta。

key_metrics.market_position: strong but challenged by NVIDIA GPU ecosystem

composite_score.confidence_reason: Very strong direct signals: company explicitly disclosed the $3.9B profit impact of depreciation life extension, with exact numbers and line-item breakdown. Server life extension from 4 to 6 years is the most aggressive policy change among all 4 annotated companies.
